segments.text: OM NAMO BHAGWATE VASUDEVAYA
OM NAMO BHAGWATE VASUDEVAYA
3 CHAPTER 18 TEXT 1
MAITREYAV VACHAM
TADEVAM AKARNAYA JALESHA BHASHITAM
TADEVAM AKARNAYA JALESHA BHASHITAM
MAHAMANASTAV VIGANAYA DURMADHAHA
MAHAMANASTAV VIGANAYA DURMADHAHA
HARER VIDITVA GATIMANGA NARADHA
HARER VIDITVA GATIMANGA NARADHA
VASATALAM NIRVISHESHAT TVARNAN VITAHA
VASATALAM NIRVISHESHAT TVARNAN VITAHA
Translation and Procurement by His Divine Grace
A.C. R.I.P.
Translation
MAITREYA CONTINUED
THE PROUD AND FALSELY GLORIOUS DAITYA
PAID LITTLE HEED TO THE WORDS OF VARUNA
O DEAR VIDURA
HE LEARNED FROM NARADA
THE WHEREABOUTS OF THE SUPREME PERSONALITY OF GODHEAD
AND HURRIDLY BETOOK HIMSELF TO THE DEPTHS OF THE OCEAN
PERPET
METEOROLISTIC WARMONGERS ARE NOT EVEN AFRAID
TO FIGHT WITH THE MIGHTIEST ENEMY
THE PERSONALITY OF GODHEAD
THE PERSONALITY OF GODHEAD
THE DEMON WAS VERY ENCOURAGED TO LEARN FROM VARUNA
THAT THERE WAS ONE FIGHTER WHO COULD ACTUALLY COMBAT HIM
AND HE WAS VERY ENTHUSIASTIC TO SEARCH OUT THE SUPREME PERSONALITY OF GODHEAD
JUST TO GIVE HIM A FIGHT
EVEN THOUGH IT WAS PREDICTED BY VARUNA
THAT BY FIGHTING WITH VISHNU
HE WOULD BECOME PREY FOR DOGS, JACKALS, AND WORDCHURDS
SINCE DEMONIAC PERSONS ARE LESS INTELLIGENT
THEY DARE TO FIGHT WITH VISHNU
WHO IS KNOWN AS AJITA
OR ONE WHO HAS NEVER BEEN CONQUERED
THUS ENTERED THE DEMONIC PERSONALITY OF GODHEAD
AND HE WAS VERY ENCOURAGED TO LEARN FROM VARUNA
SO HERE IN THESE WORDS
HEARNAKSHA IS BEING REFERRED AS FALSELY GLORIOUS DAITYA
HE WAS A DAITYA
AND HE WAS PROUD
AND FALSELY GLORIOUS
VARUNA HAD ACTUALLY TOLD HIM
THAT
VARUNA HAD ACTUALLY TOLD HIM
THERE IS ONE FIGHTER WHO CAN ACTUALLY COMBAT HIM
BECAUSE THIS DAITYA
WAS NOT AN ORDINARY DEMON
HE WAS VERY POWERFUL
AND VARUNA TOLD HIM
THAT THERE IS ONE FIGHTER
WHO CAN ACTUALLY COMBAT HIM
AND RATHER THAN
HEARING TO THE WORDS OF VARUNA
AND HE ALSO TOLD THAT
HE IS NONE OTHER THAN
THE SUPREME PERSONALITY OF GODHEAD
AND HE ALSO TOLD THAT
BY FIGHTING WITH VISHNU
HE WOULD BECOME PREY FOR DOGS,
JACALS AND VULCHERS
BUT INSTEAD OF HEEDING TO THE ADVICE OF VARUNA
NOT TO TAKE HEAD ON
THE SUPREME PERSONALITY OF GODHEAD
UNDERSTANDING THAT THE SUPREME PERSONALITY OF GODHEAD
IS AJITA
IS UNCONQUERABLE
DEFEAT IS
INEVITABLE
BUT THIS DAITHYA
OUT OF PRIDE
GOES IN SEARCH OF THE SUPREME LORD
SO THAT HE CAN FIGHT HIM
SUCH DEMONIAC PERSONS
ARE LESS INTELLIGENT
WHY THEY ARE CALLED LESS INTELLIGENT
BECAUSE
AN INTELLIGENT PERSONALITY
IS NOT A PERSONALITY
A PERSON WILL KEEP IN MIND
THE CIRCUMSTANCES
THE MIGHT OF THE ENEMY
WE KNOW INDIA AND PAKISTAN
BOTH ARE NUCLEAR NOW
BOTH ARE CAUTIOUS
INTELLIGENCE MEANS THAT
YOU ARE AWARE OF ENEMY'S STRENGTH AND WEAKNESS
AND HERE HE IS CALLED LESS INTELLIGENT
BECAUSE HE IS NOT A PERSONALITY
BECAUSE ALTHOUGH HE KNOWS
THE PERSON WHOM
HE HAS TO FIGHT
HIS SUPREME PERSONALITY OF GODHEAD
WHO IS AJITA
WHO CAN NEVER BE CONQUERED
STILL HE IS ENTHUSIASTIC TO FIGHT HIM
SO PRABHUPAD IN THE PRABHUPAD MENTIONS
METEOROLISTIC WARMONGERS
ARE NOT EVEN AFRAID
TO FIGHT WITH THE MIGHTIEST ENEMY
A CRUDE ACCOUNT OF THEM
IN A DENOUNCED EARTH
IN MOST NATIONS
THAT MIGHT NOT BE VERY RELAVANT
BUT
IN THE PAST
SOME COUNTRIES
IN AMERICA
ARE SUPPOSED TO BE VERY MIGHTY
IN TERMS OF MILITARY STRENGTH
ECONOMIC STRENGTH
AND SUPER POWER
BUT SOME COUNTRIES IN THE PAST
HAVE DARED TO RAISE THEIR VOICE
BECAUSE THE RULERS WERE IN ONE SENSE
We don't know the truth but nevertheless it appears to be. And so here Prabhupada is saying materialistic war mongers are not even afraid to fight with their mightiest enemy.
So this pride comes about originally Jeeva.
We all are amshas of the Supreme Lord and we are subordinate to Him. We are subservient to Him.
We are servants of Krishna and our constitutional position is to humbly serve the Supreme Lord, to obey the Supreme Lord, to please the Supreme Lord.
How does this pride come? When we become disconnected from the Supreme Lord, when we come under the influence of Maya,
then naturally we forget our constitutional position, we try to be the Lord, we try to be the master, we try to be the controller.
And the more your person is in Maya, the more he thinks that he is the controller, to the extent that he thinks that God is not there, I am God.
So that is what is happening to Jeevas.
In Sri Lanka here, Prabhupada one time was going with some devotees in a morning walk.
And they were walking in a garden and they saw that some portion in the garden there were green grass and small portion in the side there was some dry grass.
It was yellowish in color. Sometimes we see grass which gets dried, especially in the summer season, it becomes yellow.
Even in the Ghoshala we have that dry grass. It becomes yellowish.
So Prabhupada asked, why is this grass yellow?
So devotees started giving different explanation.
And then finally Prabhupada said, because it is disconnected from the root.
There is no supply of nutrients, water, moisture.
It is disconnected.
Just like if you cut the grass, the grass which is green, you cut it, in no time it will become yellow or it will dry up because it is disconnected from the root.
So likewise all these anomalies in our behavior, pride, lust, greed, anger, these all are symptoms of our disconnection with the Lord.
That's why they said, yasyastika, whosoever becomes a pure devotee or devotee of the Lord, all good qualities starts manifesting in his behavior.
It automatically starts manifesting. I was talking about humility.
Humility is not some artificial, some sentimental alignment of our minds.
You know, we speak very softly. We always keep our heads down.
We don't scold anyone.
You know, generally we have another, he is very humble, very soft spoken.
But genuine humility is something different from the external symptoms which we are aware of.
Prabhupada at times, he chastised his disciples.
He used harsh words.
At times he got angry.
Somebody might construe that Prabhupada was not humble, but it's not true.
The real humility, Prabhupada is an insignificant beggar.
I have no power to speak.
So genuine humility comes when one understands that I am an insignificant beggar.
I am a puppet in the hand of the Lord.
This is exactly what Hiranaksha is not having.
I am the Lord. I can fight the Lord.
I can overpower the Lord. I can defeat the Lord.
One time there was a ship.
This was earlier, many years back.
These days you have modern, you know, navigation systems.
Just like the GPRS and all that.
So there was a time when the ship, huge ship,
especially when it would be lot of fog in the ocean.
So they will have very sharp beam of light.
And that's how you can identify if somebody, some ship is coming.
From the opposite direction.
And that's why sometimes ship would change course or slow down so that there is no collision.
So one time there was a big ship moving in the water, in the ocean.
And this ship, the captain saw a beam of light coming from the opposite direction.
So he, it was evident that it may be some kind of a ship.
So because this captain was captain of a very big ship, naturally he was proud.
Generally when a person drives a big car, he is relatively more puffed up than a person,
hey rickshawala.
Because it gives him a sense of that I am big.
So the captain sent a message
that I am captain of a big ship.
He named the ship, he says turn, you turn 20 degrees.
He wanted the other person to turn 20 degrees.
So back comes the message, you turn 20 degrees.
Many times we also have, you are wrong, no you are wrong.
You know we accuse each other, nobody is willing to budge.
So he says you turn 20 degrees.
So he gets angry, he says you know who I am, I am an A class officer.
I order you, you turn 20 degrees.
So back comes the answer, I am a C class worker, you turn 20 maybe.
He gets all the more angry.
Being a worker, how dare he tell a captain of a big ship to turn 20 degrees.
He says I will get you dismissed from the job.
How dare you tell me to turn 20 degrees.
He says sir I plead you, I beg you, I am a lighthouse, you turn 20 degrees.
In earlier times there used to be lighthouse.
You know the buildings, top buildings so that it can give directions to the ships.
And seeing the lighthouse the ships could direct.
Those were earlier times, not the present times.
So he says I am from the lighthouse, you turn 20 degrees.
How can the lighthouse move?
So that way, even we, in the name of advancement, like here he is saying, falsely proud.
In present time because we have got control of things, to some extent.
We can diagnose some diseases.
We can monitor a lot of things.
We can to some extent forecast, weather forecast, we can tell when it is going to rain.
To that extent, you know we have in one way got control over things.
And this brings some kind of a pride.
And out of pride, many times we even challenge ourselves.
Or challenge God.
Or come to a conclusion that there is no God.
The more and more we are disaligned or not in alignment with God's law, God's principles which are like the lighthouse principles.
The more and more we go away from our constitutional position of being self subservient to the Lord.
Acting as a dasa, the more and more we will suffer in this material world.
The more and more we are in alignment with our constitutional position, subservient to the Lord.
The position of being a dasa, the more and more we will actually experience the bliss we want to experience.
But being conditioned soul, somehow, lording over, enjoying tendencies, very very deep rooted.
Even after becoming devotees still.
Deep within that enjoying tendency, I am the enjoyer, that false ego is so prominent.
The whole process of bhakti is somehow to give up this pride, to give up this enjoying tendency and come back to our constitutional position of being a servant of the Lord.
That's why here it's called daitya, it's a demon.
Demon doesn't mean sharp claws and horns on the head and black face.
Generally in serials which we see in television, they portray demons or villains, ugly looking.
It's a demoniac mentality, the consciousness.
The mentality of challenging the Supreme Lord.
The mentality of
not agreeing to serve the Supreme Lord.
So here we are seeing this in the behavior, the character, the words of Hirnakshan.
In Kali Yuga, we are seeing more and more in present times, although as I was mentioning earlier,
we are advancing in many fronts technologically.
In so many areas we have advanced.
There is no doubt about it.
But more and more we are advancing, the more and more we are becoming in one sense rebellious to God.
There was a time when man, naturally when you are helpless,
you tend to remember God.
The more and more we are in control of things, the more and more we think that God is obsolete, not required.
We can manage without God.
One time these four Pandavas, Yudhishthira Maharaj was not there,
the four Pandavas,
they were with Krishna
and they were having a conversation and they asked Krishna,
what is going to happen in Kali Yuga?
What will be the symptoms of people in Kali Yuga?
So Krishna instead of replying, he took four arrows and shot those arrows in four different directions
and told them, kindly go and get me those arrows.
You will get answers to your question.
So Arjuna went in one direction in search of arrow
and finally he saw the arrow and he saw a very cute cuckoo bird singing very melodiously.
Cuckoo birds, the sound is very very pleasant.
So Arjuna was, when he heard that voice, he was charmed, so to say, by that sweet little word from the cuckoo bird.
When he went near the bird, near the tree, he saw the cuckoo bird actually was eating the flesh of a live rabbit
and the rabbit was wriggling in pain.
Somehow he was surprised what kind of scene he is seeing.
Cuckoo bird, sweet little bird, supposed to sing very melodiously and here is eating flesh of a live rabbit
and the rabbit is wriggling in pain.
He immediately took the arrow and came back to Krishna.
Not able to comprehend what he has seen.
Then Bhima went in another direction.
He saw the five wells.
Four wells and the one well in between these four wells.
Each of these four wells is swimming with water.
Water is overflowing, sweet little water is overflowing from the wells
and the well in the center, the fifth well is completely dry, completely empty.
He was also surprised by this sight.
What is this?
All five wells are together, four wells are overflowing with water
and the fifth well is completely dry.
Not able to comprehend, he comes back.
Nakula goes in another direction, tries to pick up the arrow
and here he sees a cow has given birth to a baby calf or whatever calf
and after birth it seems the mother cow licks the calf till it gets clean
and keeps on licking the calf, keeps on licking.
Even though the calf has become completely clean, keeps on licking.
Finally, the cowman has to pull the calf away from the cow.
By then, the calf had become wounded.
There were wounds in the body because of excessive licking by the mother cow.
So he was also surprised, how can mother cow hurt her own calf?
And Sahadev goes in another direction.
He picks up the arrow, it's at the foothills of a hill, big mountain
and he sees from the top of the mountain a big boulder, big rock is rolling down the hill.
Big boulder.
And as it's rolling down, all the big, big trees, rocks, whichever is coming on the way, it's smashing it.
It's smashing all things which comes in its way.
And finally the boulder is coming down.
And to his surprise, when it almost reaches the down, there is small little grass and the boulder stops.
Stops rolling down and stops.
That small little grass holds the boulder.
Again he is bewildered, what's going on?
Big, big trees are uprooted and small little grass has stopped this boulder and it's not moving down.
So they all come back to Krishna and says, this is what we saw.
So Krishna explains, the first thing of the cuckoo bird singing very sweetly but eating live rabbit,
says that in Kali Yuga, so-called God-men, so-called spiritualists will speak sweet words,
will appear to be the well-wishers but they will exploit the followers.
The people who will surrender to them, this we see very often happening in Kali Yuga.
So many scams happen, so many so-called God-men, people are gullible.
Although not many are spiritually inclined, even though they are spiritually inclined, they get cheated
because the real knowledge is not there.
What is genuine spirituality is not there.
Unless we are fortunate to get in touch with a bona fide spiritual master coming in a bona fide parampara,
we will also get cheated.
Today we are here not because we are intelligent enough to discriminate who is a bona fide God-man,
it's Prabhupada's causeless mercy.
So there will be God-men who will speak sweet words but they will exploit, they cheat, they eat their so-called followers.
Then the five wells, four wells overflowing with water and the fifth well completely dry signifies that
in Kali Yuga there will be extreme disparity in distribution of wealth.
On one side we will have people who are filthy rich, we see this happening in this world,
in fact the disparity is on the rise.
It says,
90% of Americans hold some 90 or 95% of the total wealth in America.
There are billionaires, people like Bill Gates, 40, 50, 60 billion dollars.
And simultaneously in the same place you might also see people living who are completely poverty stricken.
They don't have food to eat.
The third arrow, Nakula, when he went and saw that calf, cow licking the calf, indicates that the parents,
they will be very affectionate to their children, they will show so much of affection to their children,
so much of love, and that love will actually spoil the children.
Parents also when you show love, when you exhibit qualities which is not backed by shastric understanding,
like I mentioned, humility means just to be kind, not speak harshly, not scold,
to speak always pleasing words,
to not reprimand anyone, not to get angry.
In the name of being gentle to the child, in the name of giving a good upbringing,
parents pamper their children and they get spoiled.
Sometimes they wonder, what wrong we did?
We gave everything he wanted.
Gave so much of love.
That's not enough.
Our, the way we live, the way we act in this world should be backed with right knowledge.
Just the intention is not enough.
And the last, the boulder falling from the top of the hill and all the trees getting crushed
when it's moving down, and finally one small little grass holding the boulder,
it indicates that the Kali Yuga, the character of people will degrade,
like the boulder coming from the, it will fall, the character will fall actually.
People will degrade, Kali Yuga, Doshan, Nidhe, Rajan.
But finally the boulder is stopped by a small little grass, insignificant grass.
What does that grass indicate?
Kali Yuga, Doshan, Nidhe, Rajan, Astireko, Mahaguna.
By chanting the name of Krishna, that small little sound vibration,
appears to be insignificant sound vibration,
that sound vibration can stop the degradation, that fall of character,
the degradation which is there, which we are observing in the character and behavior of Hirinaksha.
In one sense, we also like demons.
If we analyze our consciousness.
The saving grace is what can really stop us is,
if we take to the holy name, chanting of the holy names of the Lord,
then this falling from grace, we say, falling of character, it can stop.
In one sense, there was mentioned in one of the classes, many times devotees,
we take all what we are doing for granted, and sometimes grass is greener the other side,
we feel that, you know, it can be practiced outside also.
Sometimes we face some kind of discomfort.
Actually, when we are in association of devotees, when we are chanting, when we are hearing,
when we are attending the sarnaks like a dam,
these forces of Kali Yuga actually are stopped.
This dam is holding the forces of Kali Yuga,
or forces of Guna, Kala, Karma.
We don't know what all forces we have to encounter in life.
Actually, the sadhana, the chanting of the holy name, association,
Nityam Bhagavata Seva is holding that force.
The moment this dam opens, we will be inundated with Guna, Kala and Karma forces.
If we stop chanting, or for some reason the chanting gets affected,
we start chanting mechanically day after day, lose interest and all that,
then the forces of Guna, Kala, Karma will overpower us, overwhelm us,
to the extent that all these demoniac qualities will again start resurfacing us.
To the extent we are following this process very diligently,
the forces are checked at bay, and all the good qualities
will start gradually manifesting in our behavior and character.
Actually, in one sense, yes, we are demoniac,
we are Kali Yuga products, in one sense.
Varna Sankara, this word is used, Kalau Shudra Sambhava,
Shudras, in that sense.
The saving grace is the Lord's holy name.
If we take shelter of, by the mercy of a pure devotee of the Lord,
we Shudras, we in one sense, Varna Sankaras also, have a hope,
that this boulder of degradation, the Kali Yuga, which is hitting all of us,
we can be saved.
So, these are a few insights we get from this verse.
On one side, we have Rinakasha demons challenging the Supreme Lord,
and in spite of Narada telling, in spite of Varuna telling,
he is not giving heed to their instructions.
Falsely glorious Daitya paid little heed to the words of Varuna.
Now, when we hear Bhagavatam daily,
or when we are an association of devotees,
we also have Anarthas, and we will be advised.
We will be told what to do, what not to do.
Our shortcomings will be pointed out.
Our Anarthas will be pointed out.
We will be advised.
But if we are demoniac mentality,
then here it is said,
paid little heed to the words of Varuna.
Prabhupada, Krishna, they act very dynamically.
They all want us to improve, become nice, pure devotees.
From time to time, guidance will come.
Sometimes people wonder, we require a physical guru.
We are practicing spiritual life,
and the Lord, Supersoul, Chaita Guru,
can act in mysterious ways.
We can get directions from so many sources.
And if we are humble, if we are properly situated,
we will take heed to the words of,
you know, if we get some advice from some senior devotees,
some senior Vaishnavas,
we are hearing a lecture of Prabhupada,
something we hear,
we will take those words seriously.
And if we are infected,
if we are demoniac mentality,
we take no heed to.
Somebody is saying we should chant attentively.
Somebody is saying we should attend all the programs.
Where is this thing?
Chalta hai.
So,
we are very fortunate that
we have an opportunity,
although we may have this kind of mentality,
we have an opportunity to,
glorious opportunity,
to rectify,
to purify.
And once we get purified,
then as I mentioned earlier,
the more and more we are
aligned to our constitutional position
as servants of Krishna,
the more and more we will be happy.
The more and more we will be blissful.
The more and more we are disaligned,
the more and more miserable we will be.
The miseries of this material world,
guna, kala, karma,
will impact us.
So with these words we will stop here.
Granth Raj,
Himmat Bhagavatam,
Kishla Prabhupada,
